title: Federal Digital Services Reliability Act
bill_text: A BILL To modernize federal digital service infrastructure, require uptime transparency reporting, and establish incident postmortem standards.

background_brief.what_the_bill_does: Creates modernization grants and reliability standards for federal digital services, including uptime reporting and required incident postmortems.
background_brief.why_it_matters: Many high-volume public services still run on brittle legacy systems that create outages and delays for citizens.
background_brief.policy_context: Builds on prior federal IT modernization efforts but adds explicit reliability and transparency obligations.
expert_perspectives: Service reliability advocates support mandatory postmortems
Some agencies worry reporting overhead could slow delivery

title: Rural Behavioral Health Access Act
bill_text: A BILL To expand rural behavioral health tele-services and stabilize provider staffing through incentive grants.

background_brief.what_the_bill_does: Funds rural tele-behavioral health capacity and workforce incentives for shortage regions.
background_brief.why_it_matters: Rural patients face significant travel and provider barriers for mental health treatment.
background_brief.policy_context: Extends pandemic-era telehealth gains while addressing persistent clinician shortages.
expert_perspectives: Rural health groups strongly support expanded tele-services
Budget hawks question whether temporary grants should become permanent

title: Grid Interregional Reliability Planning Act
bill_text: A BILL To require interregional transmission planning standards and reliability stress testing.

background_brief.what_the_bill_does: Requires standardized interregional planning, scenario stress testing, and public reliability metrics.
background_brief.why_it_matters: Extreme weather and load growth are exposing coordination gaps between regional grid operators.
background_brief.policy_context: Complements existing FERC reliability authorities with stronger planning expectations.
expert_perspectives: Reliability experts support better cross-region planning
Some utilities oppose additional compliance and modeling burdens

title: Workforce Childcare Scheduling Act
bill_text: A BILL To support off-hour childcare pilots and local workforce-childcare coordination grants.

background_brief.what_the_bill_does: Funds pilots for evening/weekend childcare and grants for local coordination between employers and providers.
background_brief.why_it_matters: Shift workers often cannot access childcare aligned to nonstandard schedules.
background_brief.policy_context: Shifts federal support toward schedule accessibility, not only tuition affordability.
expert_perspectives: Family policy groups support off-hour pilots
Provider organizations flag staffing constraints for extended hours
related_bills: Child Care for Working Families proposals

title: Public Transit Accessibility Retrofit Act
bill_text: A BILL To accelerate ADA accessibility retrofits in legacy transit stations and fleet procurement standards.

background_brief.what_the_bill_does: Provides targeted capital funding for station retrofits, accessibility compliance upgrades, and procurement standards.
background_brief.why_it_matters: Many older transit systems remain difficult to use for riders with disabilities despite existing requirements.
background_brief.policy_context: Builds on ADA obligations with dedicated retrofit funding and milestone reporting.
expert_perspectives: Some agencies cite construction complexity and timeline risk
